tasks.title: fix gap analysis
tasks.description: it should include the original v1 courses

tasks.title: Rebuild the certification system.
tasks.description: Rebuild the certification system based upon version two courses that don't necessarily have a foundation but a dynamic pathway for the students based upon an assessment.

tasks.title: Ingest the recent edu board meeting content
tasks.description: Ingest the content and give AI the opportunity to make recommendations for future features and direction.

tasks.title: Consolidate Firebase client config into shared module
tasks.description:
firebase-helpers.js (introduced in Task #6) hardcodes the Firebase client config block (apiKey, projectId, appId, etc.), duplicating what is already present in students.html, transcript.html, and dashboard.html. Firebase client config is not a secret, but duplication creates a maintenance sync burden — a project change requires updating all copies.

Extract the config into a shared module (e.g. ai-academy/js/firebase-config.js) and import it from all consumers. Verify all pages still initialize Firebase correctly after the change.

tasks.title: Build the new cert program for v2
tasks.description: title

tasks.title: Convert courses to in person courses
tasks.description: Create a capability to convert individual classes to in person classes.

tasks.title: Student Assessment Evaluation
tasks.description: The student assessment tool is built. Evaluate the existing implementation against the May 19 EDU Board meeting transcript to ensure all discussed requirements were applied — interests + experience + knowledge inputs, employment skill mapping outputs, recommended course pathway, and any persona/feedback calibration discussed. Identify and log any gaps.

tasks.title: Student Portfolio System
tasks.description: Students accumulate artifacts from v2 artifact-development labs (web pages, apps, policies, etc.) into a shareable portfolio. Three of every eight lessons in v2 are artifact-building lessons — this system captures those outputs. Portfolio becomes interview and employer evidence. Prerequisite to the Real-World Partner Program (#17).

tasks.title: Real-World Partner Program (Nonprofit Track)
tasks.description: Structured program pairing nonprofits and community organizations with students in time-boxed project courses. The partner org provides a real problem; students build a working solution as their course artifact; the outcome goes on their portfolio. Model: CV Tech + Habitat for Humanity. Establishes market credibility and a B2B channel. Scott identified this as a potential mission shift after the May 19 EDU Board meeting. First step: identify 2-3 pilot nonprofit partners, define offer structure, and design the course format.

tasks.title: Lab AI Critical Feedback Mode
tasks.description: Add a lab configuration option to set an AI persona that gives honest, critical assessment rather than the default sycophantic praise. The persona should push back, identify what won't work, and not celebrate effort over outcomes. Susan Levaque raised this as a gap in current AI feedback loops during the May 19 EDU Board meeting — she noted AI needs to be explicitly told 'stop telling me how wonderful this is, tell me what's not going to work.'

tasks.title: AI Governance / NIST AI RMF Course
tasks.description: Build a v2 course specifically mapped to the NIST AI Risk Management Framework. Tejas Shroff identified a gap between existing AI content and AI governance/risk management expectations in enterprise and academic contexts. Scott has an existing AI governance tool with NIST AI RMF and HIPAA mapping already built — use that as primary source material. Course should address governance, risk identification, and compliance mapping.

tasks.title: Scouting America Merit Badge Content Partnership
tasks.description: Explore creating content aligned to Scouting America's new AI and cybersecurity merit badges. Tejas Shroff is a registered SME for Scouting America in this area and offered to explore getting Aesop into official merit badge materials. Reach: large youth audience, highly motivated learners, and a resume-building frame already built into merit badge culture. First step: Tejas to make an introduction; determine what content gaps exist in current merit badge materials.

tasks.title: Ladder data layer: database of record (Firestore schema + ADR)
tasks.description: Define the durable database of record for the entire Ladder system across all three pathways. Today only the Concepts ladder syncs to Firestore (learners/{id}.ladderProgress); Products and Use Cases live in localStorage only. Produce an ADR choosing Firestore as the system of record (matching the existing stack), plus a documented schema for collections: learners, completions, certifications, evidencePackets, certificationValidations, standardsEvidence, and resource/mapping tables (rungs, resources, videos, courses, products, useCases). Deliver a shared JS schema/helpers module the page apps import. Foundational: blocks 22,29,30,32.

tasks.title: Standards-evidence mapping engine (advisory candidate model)
tasks.description: Build the advisory standards-evidence layer specified in doc 15 and doc 16: whole-session transcript review produces candidate evidence spans mapped to standards/rubrics with status candidate|learner_confirmed|reviewer_verified|rejected|stale. No autonomous grades or mastery upgrades. Stores exact transcript spans, linked standard, rationale, confidence label, reviewer status. Separate instruction from evaluation. Depends on the data layer schema.

tasks.title: Extract shared certification engine module
tasks.description: The certification engine (AI examiner + independent second-model validator + 7-dimension rubric + challenge flow + evidence packet + identity assurance) exists only inside theladder/ladder-app.js. Extract it into a reusable shared module so Products and Use Cases can deliver real, defensible certifications instead of plain chat. Preserve the no-credential-without-validation guarantee. Blocks 27,28.

tasks.title: Extract shared placement/assessment engine module
tasks.description: The conversational placement assessment (PLACEMENT_REGEX completion signal, normalizePlacementSignals, grantRules, assignedTopicsForPlacement) lives only in theladder/ladder-app.js. Extract a reusable placement engine that any catalog (tiers, products, use cases) can drive with its own grant/assignment rules. Blocks 25,26.

tasks.title: Products: conversational assessment + placement
tasks.description: Add the placement engine to /theladder-products/. Assesses learner product fluency and interest, then recommends/assigns product categories and specific products, writes placement to durable learner record, and produces transcript evidence — mirroring the Concepts ladder. Consumes the shared placement module (24).

tasks.title: Use Cases: conversational assessment + placement
tasks.description: Add the placement engine to /theladder-use-cases/. Assesses learner work-pattern needs and recommends/assigns topic groups and specific use cases, writes placement to durable learner record, produces transcript evidence. Consumes shared placement module (24).

tasks.title: Products: certification engine port
tasks.description: Wire the three Products certification buttons (Certification/Expert/Master) to the real shared certification engine (23): examiner + second-model validator + rubric + challenge + evidence packet + credential record. Today they open ordinary chat and award nothing defensible.

tasks.title: Use Cases: certification engine port
tasks.description: Wire the three Use-Case certification buttons to the real shared certification engine (23). Same defensibility guarantees as Products.

tasks.title: Durable Firestore learner records for Products + Use Cases
tasks.description: Move Products and Use-Case completions and certifications out of localStorage-only into the Firestore database of record (21) so progress survives device changes and feeds transcript/credentials. Keep localStorage as offline cache.

tasks.title: Identity assurance + auth gate for Products + Use Cases
tasks.description: Port the authenticate.html / identityAssurance / 18+ adult gate / account-binding pattern from the Concepts ladder to Products and Use Cases, since their certifications are higher-stakes and currently have no identity binding.

tasks.title: Multi-language translation for Products + Use Cases
tasks.description: Extend the 9-language LADDER_UI_TRANSLATIONS / t() / data-i18n system to /theladder-products/ and /theladder-use-cases/. Currently English-only while the Concepts ladder is fully localized.

tasks.title: Surface Products + Use-Case progress in transcript + student hub
tasks.description: transcript.html, students.html, and the credential pages read only aesop-ladder-state (Concepts). Aggregate Products and Use-Case completions/certifications into a unified learner record across all three pathways.

tasks.title: Product/Use-Case credential pages + shareable links
tasks.description: Concepts has 5 credential pages; the sibling pathways have none. Add credential surfaces and shareable verification links for Product and Use-Case certifications.

tasks.title: Reconcile products catalog drift (250 to 500) + expansion filters
tasks.description: The products catalog now has 500 rows but doc 18, the page label, and the Education Focus dropdown all still say 250. Verify the page parses all 500, add the planned Workforce/Operations/Industry expansion-track filters from the expansion research, and reconcile the docs.

tasks.title: Sync Obsidian docs + changelog for Ladder parity
tasks.description: After parity ships, update docs 15/16/18/19 and 4-Changelog to reflect that Products and Use Cases now have placement, real certification, durable records, identity, translations, and learner-record surfacing.
